test_fixture_fix_disclosure: motor/registry/tests/test_motor_registry_invariants.py::test_I7_production_sha_set_without_runtime_proven_rejected relied on the live job record's promotion state being DIVERGED. The record legitimately progressed, so the test premise (not its invariant) broke. Fixed by explicitly setting promotion DIVERGED in the test fixture — the same self-contained pattern its sibling test already uses. validate.py and both schemas are untouched (L5). Validator: exit 0. Registry tests: 52 passed.

attribution_verdict.founder_decision_point: Strict doctrine (registry README: 'attribution PROVEN (deployed SHA == candidate SHA)') can never be satisfied for a mid-PR candidate because production deploys main merge commits. Options: (a) re-candidate the job artifact to the founder-approved merge a80f828f (or PR head 61ea44f2) with a doctrine note, after which attribution is provable TODAY from the existing deploy receipt + probes; (b) keep candidate defb5506 and accept MOTOR-WEB-001 can never reach RECEIPT_COMPLETE. Registry/doctrine choice is founder territory — not machine-decided.

remaining_to_close: receipt_hash + real created/executing timestamps at completion
not_done_and_not_authorized: re-candidating the job without founder decision
